tipRules.tip: Johnston: On clear-water smallmouth, use a drop shot with an 8-10 inch leader. Fish shallow (15 ft or less) targeting rock, sand, and weed -- every spot is different.
Johnston: Use a Flogger (cone-shaped surface viewing device) to see your bait, see the fish, and see the sweet spot. It shows how fish react to your bait and speeds up the process.

knowledge.insight: Johnston: Save certain previously-located fish for when you need a big bite. Start tournament days with known bed fish or saved difference-makers to build an early cushion, then go fishing.
Johnston: Don't burn too many fish -- get a limit, upgrade a little, but save fish for subsequent days. Play the long game over a multi-day tournament.
Johnston: On the St. Lawrence, fish a mix of rock, sand, and weed in 15 feet or less. Hit as many spots as you can and hope the right ones bite.

Johnston: A Flogger (cone-shaped clear-screen surface viewing device) lets you see your bait, where the fish is, and the sweet spot. You see how the fish reacts to your bait -- it speeds up the process.
